title: Integrated circuit with dielectric waveguide connector using photonic bandgap structure
abstract: An encapsulated integrated circuit package (100) includes an integrated circuit (IC) die (102). A radio frequency (RF) circuit (142) on the IC die (102) is operable to send and/or receive an RF signal (141) having a selected frequency. Encapsulation material (110) encapsulates the IC die (102). A photonic waveguide (140) couples to the RF circuit (142) and extends to an external surface (143) of the encapsulated IC (102). The photonic waveguide (140) may be formed by a photonic bandgap structure (121, 123) within the encapsulation material (110). A socket (143) may be included with the encapsulated package (100) that is coupled to an end of the photonic waveguide (140) opposite the RF circuit (142).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An encapsulated package comprising: an integrated circuit (IC) die; a radio frequency (RF) circuit fabricated on the IC die operable to send and/or receive an RF signal having a first frequency; encapsulation material encapsulating the IC die; and a photonic waveguide that couples to the RF circuit and extends to an external surface of the encapsulated package formed by a photonic structure within the encapsulation material. 2. The encapsulated package of claim 1, further comprising a socket coupled to an end of the photonic waveguide opposite the RF circuit. 3. The encapsulated package of claim 2, wherein the socket is configured to mate with a separate waveguide connector. 4. The encapsulated package of claim 2, wherein the socket is recessed into the encapsulation material. 5. The encapsulated package of claim 2, wherein the encapsulated IC has a top surface and an opposite bottom surface approximately parallel to a top surface of the IC die, and a side surface that spans between the top surface and the bottom surface; and wherein the socket is positioned on the side surface. 6. The encapsulated package of claim 2, wherein the encapsulated IC has a top surface and an opposite bottom surface approximately parallel to a top surface of the IC die, and a side surface that spans between the top surface and the bottom surface; and wherein the socket is positioned on the top surface. 7. The encapsulated package of claim 1, wherein the photonic structure is a photonic bandgap structure that includes a stop band that includes the first frequency. 8. The encapsulated package of claim 1, further comprising a leadframe with a die attach pad in which a portion of the photonic structure is located between the IC die and the die attach pad. 9. The encapsulated package of claim 7, wherein the photonic bandgap structure includes a matrix of periodically spaced nodes within the encapsulation material, wherein the encapsulation material has a first intrinsic property and the nodes have a second intrinsic property that is 

 different from the first intrinsic property. 10. The encapsulated package of claim 1, wherein the photonic structure is a photonic resonant structure that includes a pass band that includes the first frequency. 11. The encapsulated package of claim 1, wherein the photonic structure includes a diffusion of particles within the encapsulation material, wherein the encapsulation material has a first intrinsic property and the nodes have a second intrinsic property that is different from the first intrinsic property 12. The encapsulated package of claim 1, further comprising an expanded launch structure coupled to the RF circuit. 13. A method for encapsulating an integrated circuit, the method comprising: attaching an integrated circuit (IC) die to a leadframe, wherein the IC die includes a radio frequency circuit; and encapsulating the IC die with a process that forms a photonic structure within an encapsulation material to form an encapsulated package, wherein the photonic structure is configured to provide a photonic waveguide that couples to the RF circuit and extends to an external surface of the encapsulated package. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein forming the photonic structure comprises forming a matrix of periodically spaced nodes within the encapsulating material, in which the encapsulation material has a first intrinsic property and the nodes have a second intrinsic property that is different from the first intrinsic property. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein forming the photonic bandgap structure comprises: forming a first matrix of periodically spaced nodes within the encapsulating material having a first lattice constant; and forming a second matrix of periodically spaced nodes within the encapsulating material having a second lattice constant. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein forming the photonic structure comprises diffusing particles within the encapsulating material, in which the encapsulation material has a first intrinsic property and the particles have a second intrinsic property that is different from the first intrinsic property. 17. An encapsulated integrated circuit package comprising: an integrated circuit (IC) die; a radio frequency (RF) circuit fabricated on the IC die operable to send and/or receive an RF signal having a first frequency; encapsulation material encapsulating the IC die; and a PBG waveguide that couples to the RF circuit and extends to an external surface of the encapsulated IC package formed by a photonic bandgap (PBG) structure within the encapsulation material, wherein the PBG structure is a multilayer PBG structure in which each layer of the multilayer PBG structure is configured to have a photonic bandgap with a different frequency range. 18. The encapsulated IC of claim 17, wherein one of the layers of the multilayer PBG structure includes a bandgap that includes the first frequency. 19. The encapsulated IC of claim 17, wherein each layer of the multilayer photonic bandgap structure includes a matrix of periodically spaced nodes within the encapsulation material, wherein the encapsulation material has a first intrinsic property and the nodes in a first layer of the PBG structure have an intrinsic property that is different from the first intrinsic property and different from an intrinsic property of nodes in a second layer of the PBG structure. 20. The encapsulated IC of claim 17, further comprising a socket coupled to an end of the PBG waveguide opposite the RF circuit.
